start.speaker: narrator
start.text: You stand before the Heartwood with the knowledge you've gathered — from Old Maren, from the dead fields, from the forest itself. The talisman hums steadily against your chest. The roots are still, waiting.

start_2.speaker: heartwood
start_2.text: You return. The talisman carries new weight — you have walked the dead earth and listened to its silence. You have spoken with the old root-speaker. You carry understanding now, or the shape of it.

start_3.speaker: heartwood
start_3.text: Speak. What do you offer in place of blood?

options.text: The dead fields south of Ashvale — forty acres of poisoned earth that no one can farm. I'm offering them to you. Room to grow. Room to spread your roots without competing with the village.
The village will pay tribute — gold, goods, resources — to fund the forest's maintenance.
The druids will return. We'll restore the old ways — tending the forest, speaking the root-tongue, honoring the trees.
A true partnership. The forest protects the village from external threats. The village protects the forest from loggers and fire. Mutual survival.

land_response.speaker: narrator
land_response.text: The roots shudder. The bioluminescence surges — green, gold, blinding white. The entire clearing seems to inhale.

land_response_2.speaker: heartwood
land_response_2.text: Room. Room to grow. You understand. In three hundred years, no human has understood what we truly hunger for. Not blood — blood was the fuel for roots that had nowhere to reach. We are pressed against stone and settlement on every side. We cannot breathe.

land_response_3.speaker: heartwood
land_response_3.text: Forty acres. Poisoned earth. We will drink the poison and make it green. New roots, new trees, new canopy. We will grow south while Ashvale grows north. The fields you cannot farm will become forest you never need to fear.

land_response_4.speaker: heartwood
land_response_4.text: But this is not yours alone to give. The fields belong to the village — to every faction that claims a stake. The lord, the guild, the guard, the church, the farmers. All must consent. Not because we demand it, but because a pact made by one and broken by many taught us this: true agreements require every root.

consent_discussion.speaker: heartwood
consent_discussion.text: Bring us their voices. Bring us the words of every faction: 'We give this earth freely.' When all have spoken, the new pact will be written — not in blood, but in root and soil and the slow patient language of growth.

options.text: I've already spoken with them. Every faction has agreed. The fields are yours.
I'll go back and convince them. Give me time.
[LIE] They've all agreed. Every one of them.

consent_given.speaker: heartwood
consent_given.text: The roots reach out — gently, tentatively — and touch the earth at the clearing's edge. Testing. Tasting. Then they begin to move. Not toward Ashvale. South. Toward the dead fields.

consent_given_2.speaker: heartwood
consent_given_2.text: We taste their consent in the soil. The lord's reluctant agreement. The guild's calculated acceptance. The guard's wary trust. The druid's joy. Yes. Yes, this is real. This is freely given.

new_pact_formed.speaker: narrator
new_pact_formed.text: The Heartwood begins to reshape. The gnarled root mass unfolds, stretching, reaching south like a river changing course. The bioluminescence flows along the new roots — warm gold, spreading outward.

new_pact_formed_2.speaker: heartwood
new_pact_formed_2.text: The old pact is dissolved. The blood-debt is forgiven. In its place: earth for root, root for earth. No more sacrifice. No more hunger. We will grow together or not at all.

new_pact_formed_3.speaker: heartwood
new_pact_formed_3.text: The blood-root that poisoned your wells will wither within the week. Your crops will grow again within the season. The soil will heal — we will help it heal. Consider it... a gift. The first of many, if this partnership holds.

new_pact_formed_4.speaker: heartwood
new_pact_formed_4.text: One condition: send someone to speak with us each season. The old root-speaker, or another who carries the talisman. A voice between forest and field. So that silence never again breeds misunderstanding.

final_scene.speaker: narrator
final_scene.text: Old Maren kneels and places her palm on the soil. She smiles — a smile that transforms her weathered face into something radiant.

final_scene_2.speaker: old_maren
final_scene_2.text: The roots are singing. Not the old song — something new. Something I've never heard before. She turns to you, tears on her blind face. You did it. Three hundred years of blood and silence, and you ended it with forty acres of dead earth and a little understanding.

final_scene_3.speaker: narrator
final_scene_3.text: As you leave the clearing, the forest parts before you — not grudgingly, but openly. The branches form an archway. Sunlight filters through in golden columns. Behind you, the sound of new roots spreading south. In front of you, the village of Ashvale, imperfect and scarred, but alive. Still alive.

time_to_convince.speaker: heartwood
time_to_convince.text: Go. Speak to your factions. We will wait — but not long. The hunger persists until the new roots take hold. If your village cannot agree before the next new moon, the old pact reasserts. Blood or fire. Those will be the only choices left.

caught_lying.speaker: heartwood
caught_lying.text: The roots snap taut. The ground cracks beneath your feet. The bioluminescence turns blood-red.

caught_lying_2.speaker: heartwood
caught_lying_2.text: LIAR. We taste the soil where your village stands. We feel every footstep, every whispered argument, every faction that still clings to those dead fields. You bring us a lie wrapped in good intentions. This is EXACTLY what the Ashworths did. Promise and deceive. Promise and deceive.

caught_lying_3.speaker: heartwood
caught_lying_3.text: The roots recede violently. The clearing darkens.

caught_lying_4.speaker: heartwood
caught_lying_4.text: The third path is closed. You had our trust. You spent it on a lie. Blood or fire, root-walker. Those are the only languages your kind truly speaks.

wrong_offer_gold_2.speaker: heartwood
wrong_offer_gold_2.text: Gold. You offer us metal dug from our bones. We cannot eat gold. We cannot grow in gold. You understand nothing. Try again, root-walker. But our patience thins with each wrong answer.

wrong_offer_worship.speaker: heartwood
wrong_offer_worship.text: The roots sigh — a vast exhalation of air through a thousand hollow trunks.

wrong_offer_worship_2.speaker: heartwood
wrong_offer_worship_2.text: The druids. Yes, we remember them. They were kind. They understood. But their kindness did not feed our roots. Their understanding did not give us room. We need something tangible, not devotion. Try again.

partial_offer_partnership.speaker: heartwood
partial_offer_partnership.text: The roots stir with something like interest.

partial_offer_partnership_2.speaker: heartwood
partial_offer_partnership_2.text: Partnership. Protection. This is closer. You see the shape of what we need but not the substance. We do not need protection — we have endured ice ages and wildfires and a thousand human wars. What we need is simpler and harder to give. Think, root-walker. What does any living thing need most to grow?

options.text: Space. You need space. Room to spread your roots. The dead fields — that's what you want.
Water? The wells drain the water table — you need more water.
Time. You need time to recover.

wrong_guess_water.speaker: heartwood
wrong_guess_water.text: We make our own water. Rain answers to no one, least of all humans. This is not what we need. You are further from the truth now, not closer.

wrong_guess_time.speaker: heartwood
wrong_guess_time.text: We have had three hundred years of time. Time changes nothing when the walls remain the same. A tree in a box does not need more time — it needs the box removed.

final_warning.speaker: heartwood
final_warning.text: We grow tired of guessing games. You came to renegotiate but you do not understand what you are negotiating for. Leave. Walk the dead fields south of your village. Put your hand in the soil and feel how empty it is. Feel how the roots strain against stone walls that your ancestors built to pen us in. Then return. Or don't.
